## [06:09 - 09:10]

pre-launch content, that will show your positioning. In this module, we'll show you some PLC that some of our students have shown and you can see how they create positioning in their life. The number three there is teach. It's important that you just don't go on and on and on and on about the opportunity. People will get sick of that. So you can open up, you can share that opportunity, but then you do move to teaching. Again, just using myself as an example, in my first video, I definitely, I build this opportunity. I build this positioning, but then I actually go in and I teach the sideways sales letter and I teach it to a very deep degree. Then number four, you always want, this is the thing that a lot of people miss is that no matter what you're selling, there's going to be objections. People are going to have objections to what you're talking about, to objections to how their life can change, to whether it will work for them, to whether it will work in their situation. You need to talk about those objections. You can't just put your head in the sand and pretend they don't exist because there's always going to be a number of objections. There's usually going to be two to three really big ones and then a bunch of smaller ones. You don't need to raise and answer every single one of them, but you definitely need to raise and answer the top one or two. Here's a hint. Just because you answer them in this video doesn't mean then they're gone forever. You need to keep on coming back to them and continue to answer them. Just don't pretend that they don't exist. Don't ignore them. That's one of the real powers of Product Ones Formulate. It gives you these three videos plus your sales video, plus the email copy all in between to continually talk about these objections and answer them because that's one of the most effective ways to make sales is to remove the objections. And then you want to foreshadow what's coming in PLC2. You let your people know there's another video coming and something that's cool that's going to be in it and you try to hook them. This is just like TV does this really well right now. Serial TV. They do a really good job of creating these threads and letting you know and foreshadowing that something's coming. And then you want to end your video with a call to action, which is usually ask for a comment on your blog or in social media. So that's just this overall framework. You open with showing the opportunity, then you position yourself, then you teach, raise and answer objections, foreshadow the next video, and then call to action. Get people to comment.
